task_completion.description: Open the initial floorplan.dxf file in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreCAD application is open displaying a complex architectural floor plan (2-car garage), with 'floorplan.dxf' visible in the window title or tab.
The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows a layer specifically named 'Dimensions'.

task_completion.description: Create a horizontal linear dimension measuring the full width of the drawing's outermost bounding box
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new horizontal dimension line is visible on the canvas, with its left and right extension lines aligning exactly with the absolute leftmost and rightmost points of the entire floor plan structure.

task_completion.description: Position the new dimension below the drawing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The newly created full-width dimension line is placed vertically lower than all other elements of the floor plan on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Save the modified drawing as floorplan_dims.dxf
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title/tab updates to 'floorplan_dims.dxf', or a 'Save As' dialog is seen saving the file with this exact name in the /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through LibreCAD menus, toolbars, and interacting with the canvas, rather than using terminal scripts (like Python/ezdxf) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual dimension tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The dimension appears as a standard CAD dimension entity (with automatically generated text, arrows, and extension lines) rather than manually drawn separate line segments and text boxes.

integrity.description: The original floor plan remains intact
integrity.visual_evidence: The complex 2-car garage drawing is still fully visible and unmodified other than the addition of the new dimension and layer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is a genuine 2-car garage architectural floor plan containing 24 named layers, 63 existing dimension entities, and 89 text entities. The VLM should expect to see a complex, professional-looking CAD drawing. The new dimension must span the absolute outermost bounds of this entire structure.
